session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 35 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Referenced by database_session_create(), negotiate_elect_future_host(), and session_entry_destroy().

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 113 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

89   MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, 
90   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
91   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
92 } migration_state_t;

  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2311 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2311                                                                               {
2312   if (!session) {
2313     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2314     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2315   }
2316 
2317   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2318            session->participant_id[1]);
2319 
2320   // Get configured port (or use default)
2321   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2322 
2323   // Mark ourselves as host
2324   session->is_host = true;
2325 
2326   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2327   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2328     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2329         .port = host_port,
2330         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2331         .max_clients = 32,
2332         .encryption_enabled = true,
2333     };
2334 
2335     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2336     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2337       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2338       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2339     }
2340 
2341     // Start listening for new participant connections
2342     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2343     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2344       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2345       return hstart;
2346     }
2347 
2348     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2349   }
2350 
2351   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2352   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2353   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2354   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2355   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2356   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2357   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2358   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2359   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2360 
2361   if (session->acds_transport) {
2362     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement, sizeof(announcement), 0);
2363     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2364              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2365   }
2366 
2367   // Mark migration complete
2368   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2369 
2370   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2371   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2372 
2373   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2374 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2188 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2188                                                                                    {
2189   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2190     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2191     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2192   }
2193 
2194   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2195   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2196     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2197     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2198       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2199     }
2200     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2201     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2202   }
2203 
2204   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2205   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2206   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2207     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2208   }
2209 
2210   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2211   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2212     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2213   }
2214 
2215   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2216 
2217   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2218   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2219     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2220     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2221       // Ping timed out
2222       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2223       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2224       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2225                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2226 
2227       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2228       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2229         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2230         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2231       }
2232     }
2233   }
2234 
2235   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2236   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2237   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2238     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2239     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2240     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2241       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2242       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2243         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2244         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2245         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2246                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2247       } else {
2248         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2249         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2250         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2251           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2252           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2253         }
2254       }
2255     }
2256   }
2257 
2258   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2259 }

Session Session context

2376                                                                                          {
2377   if (!session) {
2378     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2379     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2380   }
2381 
2382   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2383            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2384 
2385   // Destroy old participant context if present
2386   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2387     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2388     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2389   }
2390 
2391   // Create new participant context for the future host
2392   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2393       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2394       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2395       .enable_audio = true,
2396       .enable_video = true,
2397       .encryption_enabled = true,
2398   };
2399 
2400   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2401   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2402     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2403     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2404   }
2405 
2406   // Attempt connection to future host
2407   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2408   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2409     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2410     return pconn;
2411   }
2412 
2413   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2414 
2415   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2416   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2417   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2418   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2419 
2420   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2421            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2422 
2423   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2424   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2425 
2426   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2427   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2428 
2429   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2430 }

  discovery_config_t * config)

Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 51 of file

51                                                                                 {
52   if (!config) {
53     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
54     return NULL;
55   }
56 
57   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
58   if (!session) {
59     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
60     return NULL;
61   }
62 
63   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
64   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
65   session->acds_transport = NULL;
66   session->peer_manager = NULL;
67   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
68   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
69   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
70   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
71   session->stun_servers = NULL;
72   session->stun_count = 0;
73   session->turn_servers = NULL;
74   session->turn_count = 0;
75 
76   // Initialize host liveness detection
77   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
78   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
80   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
81   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
82   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
83   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
84 
85   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
86            session->host_ctx);
87 
88   // Load or generate identity key
89   char identity_path[256];
90   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
91   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
93     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
94       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
95       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
96       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
97       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
98         // Save for future use
99         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
100       }
101     }
102   }
103 
104   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
105     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
106     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
107     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
108   } else {
109     char fingerprint[65];
110     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
111     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
112   }
113 
114   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
115   const options_t *opts = options_get();
116   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
117     // Load all identity keys from options
118     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
119       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
120       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
121       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
122       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
123         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
124         char fingerprint[65];
125         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
126         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
127       } else {
128         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
129       }
130     }
131     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
132     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
133   } else {
134     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
135     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
136     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
137   }
138 
139   // ACDS connection info
140   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
141     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
142   } else {
143     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
144   }
145   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
146 
147   // Session string (if joining)
148   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
149     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
150     session->is_initiator = false;
151   } else {
152     session->is_initiator = true;
153   }
154 
155   // Callbacks
156   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
157   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
158   session->on_error = config->on_error;
159   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
160   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
161   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
162 
163   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
164             session->acds_port);
165 
166   return session;
167 }

170   if (!session) {
171     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
172     return;
173   }
174 
175   // Close ACDS connection
176   if (session->acds_transport) {
177     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
178     session->acds_transport = NULL;
179   }
180   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
181     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
182     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
183   }
184 
185   // Destroy host context if active
186   if (session->host_ctx) {
187     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
188     session->host_ctx = NULL;
189   }
190 
191   // Destroy participant context if active
192   if (session->participant_ctx) {
193     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
194     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
195   }
196 
197   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
198   if (session->peer_manager) {
199     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
200     session->peer_manager = NULL;
201   }
202 
203   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
204   if (session->stun_servers) {
205     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
206     session->stun_servers = NULL;
207     session->stun_count = 0;
208   }
209 
210   if (session->turn_servers) {
211     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
212     session->turn_servers = NULL;
213     session->turn_count = 0;
214   }
215 
216   SAFE_FREE(session);
217 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect
  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2261 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2261                                                                                                                      {
2262   if (!session) {
2263     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2264     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2265   }
2266 
2267   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2268 
2269   // Initialize migration context
2270   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2271   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2272   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2273   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2274 
2275   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2276   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2277   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2278   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2279   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2280   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2281   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2282 
2283   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2284 
2285   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2286   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2287     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2288     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2289     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2290     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2291     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2292   }
2293 
2294   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2295   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2296   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2297 
2298   // Check if I am the future host
2299   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2300     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2301     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2302     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2303   } else {
2304     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2305     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2306              session->ring.future_host_port);
2307     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2308   }
2309 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1484 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1484                                                                                               {
1485   if (!session) {
1486     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1487   }
1488 
1489   // Handle state-specific processing
1490   switch (session->state) {
1491   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1492     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1493     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1494       // Set socket timeout
1495       if (socket_set_timeout(session->acds_socket, timeout_ns) == 0) {
1496         packet_type_t type;
1497         void *data = NULL;
1498         size_t len = 0;
1499         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1500 
1501         // Try to receive a packet (non-blocking with timeout)
1502         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1503         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1504           if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1505             log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1506             set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1507             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1508           } else {
1509             // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1510             log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1511             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1512           }
1513         } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1514           // Timeout is normal, just return
1515         } else {
1516           log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1517         }
1518       }
1519     } else {
1520       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1521       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1522     }
1523     break;
1524   }
1525 
1526   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1527     // Handle NAT negotiation
1528     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1529       // Initialize negotiation
1530       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1531       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1532 
1533       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS immediately (let peer receive it)
1534       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1535       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1536         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1537         // Don't fail - we'll try again next iteration
1538       }
1539     }
1540 
1541     // Try to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS (non-blocking with short timeout)
1542     if (!session->negotiate.peer_quality_received) {
1543       asciichat_error_t recv_result = receive_network_quality_from_acds(session);
1544       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1545         // Got peer quality - proceed to election
1546         log_info("Received peer NETWORK_QUALITY, proceeding to host election");
1547       } else if (recv_result != ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1548         // Real error (not just timeout)
1549         log_debug("Error receiving NETWORK_QUALITY: %d (this might be normal if peer not ready)", recv_result);
1550       }
1551     }
1552 
1553     // Check if we have both qualities and can determine host
1554     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1555       // Both sides have exchanged NAT quality - determine the winner
1556       log_info("Both NAT qualities received, determining host...");
1557       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1558       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1559         // Store result
1560         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1561 
1562         if (session->is_host) {
1563           // We won the negotiation - become the host
1564           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1565                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1566                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1567           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1568           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1569           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1570           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1571         } else {
1572           // They won - we'll be a participant
1573           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1574                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1575                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1576                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1577           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1578           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1579           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1580         }
1581       } else {
1582         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1583         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1584       }
1585     } else {
1586       // Still waiting for peer quality - sleep briefly to avoid busy loop
1587       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1588     }
1589     break;
1590   }
1591 
1592   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1593     // Start hosting
1594     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1595       // Create host context with configured port
1596       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1597 
1598       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1599           .port = host_port,
1600           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1601           .max_clients = 32,
1602           .encryption_enabled = true,
1603       };
1604 
1605       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1606       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1607         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1608         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1609         break;
1610       }
1611 
1612       // Start listening
1613       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1614       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1615         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1616         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1617         break;
1618       }
1619 
1620       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1621     }
1622 
1623     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1624     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1625       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1626     }
1627 
1628     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1629     break;
1630   }
1631 
1632   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1633     // Connect to host as participant
1634 
1635     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1636     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1637       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1638       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1639         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1640         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1641         break;
1642       }
1643 
1644       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1645       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1646         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1647                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1648 
1649         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1650         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1651             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1652 
1653         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1654           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1655           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1656           break;
1657         }
1658 
1659         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1660         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1661         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1662       }
1663 
1664       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1665       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1666         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1667         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1668 
1669         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1670           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1671                     timeout_ms);
1672 
1673           // Check if we have retries remaining
1674           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1675           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1676             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1677             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1678 
1679             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1680                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1681 
1682             // Wait for backoff period
1683             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1684 
1685             // Destroy old peer manager
1686             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1687             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1688 
1689             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1690             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1691             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1692               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1693               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1694 
1695               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1696               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1697                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1698                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1699                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1700               }
1701 
1702               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1703             }
1704 
1705             // Reset connection state for retry
1706             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1707             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1708 
1709             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1710                      max_attempts + 1);
1711           } else {
1712             // No more retries - connection failed
1713             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1714 
1715             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1716             char error_msg[256];
1717             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1718                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1719             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1720 
1721             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1722             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1723               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1724               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1725               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1726             }
1727 
1728             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1729           }
1730         }
1731       }
1732 
1733       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1734       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1735       if (session->acds_transport) {
1736         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1737         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1738         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1739         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1740 
1741         fd_set readfds;
1742         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1743         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1744 
1745         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1746         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1747           // Data available to read
1748           packet_type_t type;
1749           void *data = NULL;
1750           size_t len = 0;
1751           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1752 
1753           asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1754 
1755           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1756             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1757             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1758             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1759           } else {
1760             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1761           }
1762         }
1763       }
1764 
1765       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1766       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1767       break;
1768     }
1769 
1770     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1771       // Create participant context for this session
1772       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1773           .address = session->host_address,
1774           .port = session->host_port,
1775           .enable_audio = true,
1776           .enable_video = true,
1777           .encryption_enabled = true,
1778       };
1779 
1780       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1781       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1782         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1783         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1784         break;
1785       }
1786 
1787       // Attempt connection
1788       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1789       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1790         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1791         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1792         break;
1793       }
1794 
1795       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1796 
1797       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1798       // Get the socket from participant context
1799       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1800       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1801         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1802         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1803         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1804         break;
1805       }
1806 
1807       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1808       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1809       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1810       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1811       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1812       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1813       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1814       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1815       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1816 
1817       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1818       if (result != 0) {
1819         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1820         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1821         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1822         break;
1823       }
1824 
1825       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1826       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1827       packet_type_t packet_type;
1828       void *payload = NULL;
1829       size_t payload_len = 0;
1830 
1831       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1832       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1833         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1834         if (payload) {
1835           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1836         }
1837         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1838         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1839         break;
1840       }
1841 
1842       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1843         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1844         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1845         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1846         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1847         break;
1848       }
1849 
1850       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1851       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1852       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1853 
1854       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1855         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1856         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1857         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1858         break;
1859       }
1860 
1861       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1862                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1863 
1864       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1865       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1866       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1867       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1868       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1869       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1870       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1871       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1872       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1873       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1874 
1875       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1876       if (result != 0) {
1877         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1878         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1879         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1880         break;
1881       }
1882 
1883       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1884       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1885     }
1886 
1887     // Check if connection is established
1888     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1889     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1890     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1891               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1892 
1893     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1894       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1895       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1896     }
1897 
1898     if (is_connected) {
1899       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1900       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1901     } else {
1902       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1903                 "is_connected=%d)",
1904                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1905     }
1906     break;
1907   }
1908 
1909   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1910     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1911     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
1912       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1913       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1914 
1915       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1916 
1917       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1918         // Data available to read
1919         packet_type_t type;
1920         void *data = NULL;
1921         size_t len = 0;
1922         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1923 
1924         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1925 
1926         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1927           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1928           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1929           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1930         }
1931       }
1932     }
1933 
1934     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1935     if (!session->is_host) {
1936       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1937       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1938       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1939         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1940         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1941 
1942         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1943         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1944         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1945           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1946           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1947           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1948           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1949         }
1950 
1951         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1952         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1953         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1954       }
1955     }
1956     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1957     break;
1958 
1959   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1960     // Host migration in progress
1961     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1962     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1963       // Migration completed successfully
1964       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1965       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1966     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
1967       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
1968       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
1969       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1970       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1971     }
1972     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1973     break;
1974 
1975   default:
1976     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
1977     break;
1978   }
1979 
1980   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1981 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1296 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1296                                                                         {
1297   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1298 
1299   if (!session) {
1300     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1301   }
1302 
1303   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1304            session->session_string);
1305 
1306   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1307   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1308     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1309     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1310   }
1311 
1312   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1313   // Connect to ACDS
1314   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1315   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1316     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1317     return result;
1318   }
1319   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1320 
1321   // Check again after connection
1322   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1323     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1324     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1325   }
1326 
1327   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1328   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1329   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1330   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1331 
1332   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1333   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1334     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1335     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1336     return result;
1337   }
1338 
1339   // Store identity key in handshake context
1340   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1341   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1342   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1343   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1344 
1345   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1346   {
1347     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1348     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1349     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1350     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1351 
1352     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1353     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1354       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1355       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1356       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1357                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1358       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1359     }
1360 
1361     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1362       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1363                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1364       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1365       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1366       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1367     }
1368 
1369     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1370     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1371     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1372 
1373     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1374     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1375     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1376     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1377       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1378       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1379       return result;
1380     }
1381     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1382   }
1383 
1384   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1385   {
1386     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1387     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1388     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1389     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1390 
1391     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1392     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1393       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1394       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1395       return result;
1396     }
1397 
1398     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1399     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1400 
1401     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1402       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1403       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1404       return result;
1405     }
1406   }
1407 
1408   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1409   {
1410     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1411     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1412     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1413     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1414 
1415     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1416     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1417       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1418       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1419       return result;
1420     }
1421 
1422     result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1423     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1424 
1425     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1426       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1427       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1428       return result;
1429     }
1430   }
1431 
1432   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1433   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1434   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1435   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1436     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1437     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1438     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1439     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1440 
1441     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1442     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1443       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1444       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1445       return result;
1446     }
1447 
1448     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1449     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1450 
1451     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1452       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1453       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1454       return result;
1455     }
1456   } else {
1457     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1458   }
1459 
1460   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1461 
1462   // Check again after handshake
1463   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1464     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1465     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1466   }
1467 
1468   // Create or join session
1469   if (session->is_initiator) {
1470     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1471     result = create_session(session);
1472     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1473              session->participant_ctx, result);
1474     return result;
1475   } else {
1476     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1477     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1478     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1479              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1480     return result;
1481   }
1482 }

void
  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)


Stop the discovery session.


Parameters


Session Session context

1983                                                           {
1984   if (!session) {
1985     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1986     return;
1987   }
1988 
1989   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
1990 
1991   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
1992   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
1993     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
1994     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
1995     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1996     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1997 
1998     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg), 0);
1999   }
2000 
2001   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
2002 }
